headline: Why 80% of New Housing Is Unaffordable for 80% of Malaysians
context: The median household earns RM6,338 per month, but 78% of new housing launched in 2024 was priced above RM500,000.

cards.big: Developers build luxury. Malaysians need affordable.
cards.sub: The mismatch is not accidental — it is structural.
Without margin mandates or cross-subsidy enforcement, developers build what pays.

cards.big: State affordable housing quotas range from 20% to 40%.
cards.sub: Compliance mechanism: self-reporting. Penalty for non-compliance: project delay, not cancellation.

cards.big: 310,000 affordable units in the pipeline. 74,000 completed since 2019.
cards.sub: At current rates, the backlog clears in 2041.

cards.big: This is not a market failure. The market is working as designed.
cards.sub: The incentive structure rewards exactly what it produces.

cards.big: Housing is priced by what developers choose to build, not what people need. Fix the incentives or accept the outcome.
